코퍼스 — 015_reverse-engineering/ (심화)

015_reverse-engineering/00_reverse_evidence_policy.md

Reverse Engineering Evidence Policy

이 축은 설치 help parity 문서의 다음 단계다. 기존 00~14 축은 주로 help/config/local-doc 표면을 비교했고, 이 축은 설치된 바이너리와 번들에서 복원 가능한 구조를 따로 기록한다.

Evidence Levels

Level 의미 사용할 수 있는 주장
installed-help --help, public command output, version output 명령/플래그/공개 subcommand가 존재한다
safe-local-doc 설치 패키지의 docs, changelog, examples, manifest 문서화된 schema/경로/기능 설명이 있다
bundle-key minified bundle에 남은 module key, source path, route name, proto name 내부 모듈/라우터/프로토/컴포넌트가 존재한다
binary-string native binary strings, file, otool, codesign, nm crate/package/protocol/tool/evaluator 이름이 embedded되어 있다
runtime-trace synthetic project에서 실제 명령을 실행한 결과 동작 semantics, precedence, permission enforcement를 확인했다
private-artifact-name 비공개 상태 파일의 경로/개수만 관찰 artifact가 존재한다. 내용/schema 주장은 금지

/Users/jun/Developer/codex/015_reverse-engineering/33_source_deobfuscation_inventory.md further separates source-like recovery levels. In this corpus, Cursor reaches preserved module-key recovery, while Grok/Kiro/AGY mostly expose native source-path, handler, protobuf, and runtime-trace evidence. Do not call this "full source recovered" unless a real source map or readable source archive is present.

Hard Boundary

역분석의 목적은 제품 구조 복원이지 사용자 상태 추출이 아니다. 다음은 내용 읽기 금지다.

  • auth/token/cookie/credential/login data
  • raw transcript, chat history, conversation DB/PB
  • logs and terminal captures
  • browser profile state, local storage, IndexedDB, session storage
  • memory/goal runtime stores
  • screenshots and browser recordings
  • private mcp.json, settings, config values unless explicitly redacted

비공개 artifact는 “존재한다”까지만 쓴다. 필드/schema/content 주장은 public docs, source/bundle evidence, sanitized fixture, synthetic runtime trace가 있을 때만 쓴다.

Installed CLI version pin (2026-06-27)

Single source of truth for pinned ay/cr/gr/ki binaries:

  • /Users/jun/Developer/codex/015_reverse-engineering/36_installed_cli_version_manifest.md

WP5 trace shards (38–41) extend hubs without changing evidence-level definitions above.

Current Upgrade Scope

2026-06-27 pass — installed CLI practical RE (goal 4e94e12d-a9b):

Prefix Target Evidence kind
ay /Users/jun/.local/bin/agy v1.0.12+ help, changelog, strings; 38_ay_project_flags_trace.md
cr cursor-agent 2026.06.26-7079533 bundle string literals, help; 37, 39_cr_automations_help_trace.md
gr grok-0.2.67-macos-aarch64 inspect JSON, strings; 40_gr_0267_inspect_refresh.md
ki Kiro CLI.app 2.5.0 help surfaces; 41_ki_help_surface_trace.md

2026-05-31 pass에서 새로 역분석한 대상 (historical baseline):

Prefix Target Evidence kind
gr /Users/jun/.grok/downloads/grok-0.2.14-macos-aarch64, /Users/jun/.grok/bundled, /Users/jun/.grok/docs/user-guide Rust Mach-O strings, crate paths, ACP methods, bundled agents/roles/personas/skills
cr /Users/jun/.local/share/cursor-agent/versions/2026.05.28-418efe5 Webpack module keys, route extraction, proto module names, native helper inventory
ki /Applications/Kiro CLI.app, /Users/jun/.local/bin/kiro-cli* signed universal Mach-O binaries, Rust source-path strings, WebKit resources, dashboard/autocomplete assets
ay /Users/jun/.local/bin/agy, /Users/jun/.gemini/antigravity-cli Go/native strings, protobuf trajectory/cascade names, local cache/config metadata, helper binaries

Reading Rule For Existing Docs

When a 00~14 page says “없음” or “지원하지 않음”, interpret it as “installed help에서 노출되지 않음” unless this 15_reverse-engineering axis confirms absence from bundle/binary evidence too.

When a page states runtime semantics from config/cache observation, this axis upgrades or downgrades the claim:

  • bundle-key or binary-string confirms a module exists.
  • runtime-trace is still required for exact precedence, scheduling, isolation, merge, and permission behavior.

015_reverse-engineering/15_ay_reverse.md

AGY CLI Reverse Evidence

Practical RE evidence matrix

Claim Evidence level Source
Public flags / subcommands installed-help agy --help, agy changelog
Internal modules / handlers binary-string 15_ay_reverse.md, Mach-O strings on pinned agy
CLI activation / routing runtime-trace 31_agy_print_activation_trace.md, 32_agy_agentapi_route_trace.md
Auth-session ceiling runtime-trace (boundary) 43_ay_auth_session_boundary_trace.md
Project / permission launch flags installed-help + changelog 38_ay_project_flags_trace.md
Version pin installed-help 36_installed_cli_version_manifest.md

Version pin: see 36_installed_cli_version_manifest.md — agy 1.0.12 (changelog through 1.0.13 as of 2026-06-27).

Binary Identity

Field Evidence
Binary /Users/jun/.local/bin/agy
Version agy changelog → 1.0.13 latest entry; installed binary mtime 2026-06-27; prior pass cited 1.0.3
Type Mach-O 64-bit executable arm64
Size about 140 MB
Code signature Developer ID Application: Google LLC, identifier cli, hardened runtime
Linked frameworks/libs libresolv, CoreFoundation, Security, CoreServices, Foundation, libSystem

The helper /Users/jun/.gemini/antigravity-cli/bin/agentapi is a POSIX shell wrapper that execs /Users/jun/.local/bin/agy agentapi "$@". /Users/jun/Developer/codex/015_reverse-engineering/32_agy_agentapi_route_trace.md narrows this: the wrapper exists and binary strings expose Agent API handlers, but direct no-context agy agentapi --help falls back to top-level help and agy help agentapi reports unknown subcommand.

The helper /Users/jun/.gemini/antigravity-cli/bin/webm_encoder is a native arm64 binary with VP9 WebM and Chrome DevTools/page-capture strings, likely used for browser/screencast capture.

Local State Layout

Path Classification Notes
/Users/jun/.gemini/antigravity-cli/cache safe-local-doc subset changelog, onboarding/update metadata
/Users/jun/.gemini/antigravity-cli/mcp local cache path descriptor cache exists; schema claims need public/binary/synthetic evidence
/Users/jun/.gemini/antigravity-cli/settings.json conditional/private path exists; field values are not cited as product evidence
/Users/jun/.gemini/antigravity-cli/keybindings.json conditional/private path exists; field values are not cited as product evidence
/Users/jun/.gemini/antigravity-cli/conversations private-content denied protobuf conversation store; not decoded
/Users/jun/.gemini/antigravity-cli/log private-content denied not read
/Users/jun/.gemini/antigravity-cli/history.jsonl private-content denied not read
/Users/jun/.gemini/antigravity-cli/brain/*/.system_generated mixed/private pattern contents avoided; not used for schema/content claims

Recovered Architecture

Binary strings and local cache imply a Go/native runtime with these first-class surfaces:

Area Evidence
Trajectory trajectory.proto, trajectory_steps.proto, TrajectoryStore, CortexTrajectory, CascadeTrajectorySummary
Cascade CascadeManager, CascadeConfig, CascadeRunState, StartCascade, CancelCascadeInvocation, ForceStopCascadeTree
MCP Go MCP SDK path, ListTools, CallTool, ListResources, ReadResource, ListPrompts, GetPrompt, ToggleMcpServer, RefreshMcpServers, CompleteMcpOAuth
Browser capture BrowserSubagentHandler, StartScreenRecording, CaptureScreenshot, GetChromeDevtoolsMcpUrl, AGY_BROWSER_WS_URL, webm_encoder
Subagents SubagentSpec, InvokeSubagentHandler, BrowserSubagentHandler, KillSubagent, keybindings subagent.approve_fast, subagent.jump_to_waiting
Agent API installed agentapi wrapper, binary handlers for send-message, new-conversation, get-conversation-metadata, generated events/ strings, and prompt text saying background sidecars can use the Agent API CLI
Plugins plugin.json, registry.json, plugin-*.zip, AgentPlugin, InstallCascadePlugin, GetCascadePluginById, OpenConfigurePluginsPage
Knowledge KnowledgeBaseService, GetKnowledgeItems, GetUserMemories, UpdateCascadeMemory, connector ingest names for Slack/Jira/Google Drive/GitHub
Sandbox proceed-in-sandbox, sandboxproxy, Allow sandbox bypass for command execution?, sandbox not available with IDE command terminal

This overturns several installed-help-only negatives. AGY does not expose public mcp or subagent command groups, but the binary clearly contains MCP, subagent, browser, plugin, knowledge, and sandbox subsystems.

MCP Evidence

Local descriptor cache paths observed:

  • /Users/jun/.gemini/antigravity-cli/mcp/context7/query-docs.json
  • /Users/jun/.gemini/antigravity-cli/mcp/context7/resolve-library-id.json
  • /Users/jun/.gemini/antigravity-cli/mcp/context7/instructions.md

Binary/config migration evidence:

  • server cannot specify both command and serverUrl
  • migrated config path config/mcp_config.json
  • changelog notes legacy mcp_config.json path mismatch fix

Plugin And Skill Evidence

The installed changelog confirms:

  • version 1.0.1: plugin discovery for skills and agents
  • version 1.0.1: proceed-in-sandbox tool permission mode
  • version 1.0.2: plugin installation path moved to shared ~/.gemini/config/
  • version 1.0.2: fallback skill discovery fixed
  • version 1.0.12: /credits and UseG1Credits

2026-06-27 refresh (changelog safe-local-doc, 1.0.12–1.0.13):

  • 1.0.12: --project, --new-project; OSC8 terminal hyperlinks; permission merge priority (~/.gemini/config/projects/ over global settings); reverse diff cycling (shift+n); enterprise TLS AES-NI compile fix.
  • 1.0.13: strict vs regex: opt-in for “Always Approve” rules; relaxed redirection checks for safe commands; skill slash-prefix rendering fix; unified UI transition sync for overlays.

Binary strings add plugin manifests, registry archives, plugin install/delete/open-config flows, and agent plugin templates.

Synthetic plugin validation in /Users/jun/Developer/codex/015_reverse-engineering/22_agy_plugin_manifest_trace.md confirms more than binary strings:

  • plugin.json requires name; metadata such as version, description, displayName, and author is accepted but not required in this trace.
  • skills/trace/SKILL.md is processed as a skill.
  • agents/trace.md, agents/trace.json, and agents/trace/agent.json are all processed as agents.
  • commands/trace.md is processed and converted to a skill.
  • hooks/trace.json is processed as a hook.
  • top-level mcp_config.json with mcpServers is processed as MCP configuration.
  • plugin install <local-dir> writes to temp ~/.gemini/config/plugins/<name> and records source: local-install in import_manifest.json / plugin list.

/Users/jun/Developer/codex/015_reverse-engineering/31_agy_print_activation_trace.md adds the current no-account activation boundary: after installing a temp plugin with skill, agent, and MCP config, agy --print starts the language server, creates temp project metadata, initializes CLI store/backend with cascade/code-assist, then stops at OAuth timeout before any model response or plugin tool activation can be observed.

Documentation Consequences

  • /Users/jun/Developer/codex/000_overview/00_ay_overview.md should say public help is narrow but binary surface is broad.
  • /Users/jun/Developer/codex/005_subagents-collab/05_ay_subagent.md should replace “not confirmed” with “not public, but binary contains subagent handlers and keybindings.”
  • /Users/jun/Developer/codex/009_mcp-orchestration/09_ay_mcp.md should distinguish “no public agy mcp command” from first-class MCP runtime evidence.
  • /Users/jun/Developer/codex/010_memory-pipeline/10_ay_memory.md should document trajectory/cascade/brain/knowledge as separate surfaces while keeping conversation .pb content off-limits.
  • /Users/jun/Developer/codex/014_web-search/14_ay_web-search.md should say browser/MCP/search capability is binary-visible, but exact public CLI exposure remains runtime-trace pending.

Remaining Runtime Trace Gaps

  • Exact agentapi command activation context, JSON schema, output schema, and stability guarantees. The wrapper and binary handlers are confirmed, but normal public no-context invocation does not expose subcommand help.
  • Exact MCP load/refresh/call behavior, config precedence, OAuth flow, and standalone CLI tool exposure.
  • Exact subagent invocation path, scheduling/isolation, and whether browser/knowledge subagents require IDE-linked context.
  • Exact Cascade/Trajectory protobuf schemas from public or sanitized sources.
  • Exact post-auth plugin activation behavior in a live model/cascade session, hook lifecycle names, and marketplace trust model.
  • Exact sandbox bypass evaluator and workspace trust interactions.
  • Whether browser/search tools are available in standalone CLI sessions or only IDE-linked contexts.

Closed in 38_ay_project_flags_trace.md (2026-06-27): --project, --new-project installed-help; 1.0.12–1.0.13 permission/changelog claims (regex: opt-in, project config precedence).

015_reverse-engineering/31_agy_print_activation_trace.md

AGY Print Activation Trace

This pass used a disposable temp HOME, temp workspace, and temp local plugin. It did not read private Antigravity config, private conversations, or the user's real logs. It did not complete OAuth or run a model response.

Follow-up: authenticated local print-mode behavior is now covered by 44_ay_authenticated_print_smoke_trace.md. That newer runtime trace confirms fresh agy -p, exact --conversation <uuid> -p resume, transcript sentinel anchoring, and native checkpoint insertion through cli-jaw's synthetic smoke harness.

Fixture

  • temp HOME: /tmp/agy-plugin-activation.ESFveG/home
  • temp workspace: /tmp/agy-plugin-activation.ESFveG/ws
  • temp plugin: /tmp/agy-plugin-activation.ESFveG/plugin
  • isolated npm cache for the synthetic MCP command: /tmp/agy-plugin-activation.ESFveG/npm-cache
  • log file: /tmp/agy-plugin-activation.ESFveG/agy.log

Plugin files:

  • plugin.json with name, version, and description
  • skills/trace/SKILL.md
  • agents/trace.md
  • mcp_config.json with mcpServers.everything pointing to npx -y @modelcontextprotocol/server-everything

Validate / Install / List

agy plugin validate <plugin> returned:

[ok] /tmp/agy-plugin-activation.ESFveG/plugin
✔ skills      : 1 processed
✔ agents      : 1 processed
- commands    : skipped (not found)
✔ mcpServers  : 1 processed
- hooks       : skipped (not found)

agy plugin install <plugin> returned the same processed counts.

agy plugin list in the temp HOME returned:

{
  "imports": [
    {
      "name": "trace-plugin",
      "source": "local-install",
      "components": ["installed"]
    }
  ]
}

Installed files under temp HOME included:

  • /tmp/agy-plugin-activation.ESFveG/home/.gemini/config/import_manifest.json
  • /tmp/agy-plugin-activation.ESFveG/home/.gemini/config/plugins/trace-plugin/plugin.json
  • /tmp/agy-plugin-activation.ESFveG/home/.gemini/config/plugins/trace-plugin/agents/trace.md
  • /tmp/agy-plugin-activation.ESFveG/home/.gemini/config/plugins/trace-plugin/mcp_config.json
  • /tmp/agy-plugin-activation.ESFveG/home/.gemini/config/projects/0fb8cc35-513f-4b79-8746-7973bc7d3b6b.json

The installed plugin-level MCP file preserved:

{"mcpServers":{"everything":{"command":"/Users/jun/.nvm/versions/node/v24.14.1/bin/npx","args":["-y","@modelcontextprotocol/server-everything"],"env":{"npm_config_cache":"/tmp/agy-plugin-activation.ESFveG/npm-cache"}}}}

Print Mode Boundary

Command shape:

HOME=/tmp/agy-plugin-activation.ESFveG/home /Users/jun/.local/bin/agy --log-file /tmp/agy-plugin-activation.ESFveG/agy.log --print-timeout 20s --print "Do not use tools. Say TRACE_OK."

Observed stdout showed only a generic authentication-required prompt, a wait-for-authentication message, an authorization-code prompt, and an authentication timeout. The OAuth URL itself is not recorded here because it contains per-run state.

Synthetic Temp Log Evidence

The synthetic temp log, written under the disposable temp HOME for this trace only, showed startup lifecycle phases before the auth timeout. This is not a private user log and does not cite the user's real AGY log directory.

  • language server process started and listened on random localhost HTTPS and HTTP ports
  • migration marker under temp ~/.gemini/config/.migrated was created
  • code-assist/model/experiment polling failed because the temp HOME was not logged in
  • CLI mode launched
  • a temp project was created for /tmp/agy-plugin-activation.ESFveG/ws
  • project metadata was written under temp ~/.gemini/config/projects/<uuid>.json
  • CLI app data directory resolved to temp ~/.gemini/antigravity-cli
  • CLI server backend was created with product=antigravity, cascadeManager=true, and codeAssist=true
  • print mode started, then silent auth failed and OAuth was triggered
  • auth timed out and temp-session shutdown ran

No AGY process remained running after the trace.

Conclusions

  • Plugin validation and install are confirmed in a temp HOME immediately before print-mode startup.
  • AGY print mode initializes language-server, project, CLI store, cascade/code-assist backend, and auth flow before a model response.
  • The trace confirms the auth boundary for live plugin activation: without completing OAuth, model/cascade execution and MCP tool activation cannot be observed.
  • The installed plugin files and project metadata are durable temp-HOME artifacts, but this trace does not prove that the installed skill, agent, or MCP server entered model-facing context.

Remaining AGY gaps are now narrower: post-auth plugin activation, MCP protocol startup from plugin mcp_config.json, hook lifecycle, subagent scheduling, and sandbox/browser/search availability inside a real session.

015_reverse-engineering/43_ay_auth_session_boundary_trace.md

AGY Auth-Session Boundary Trace

Documents the authenticated-session ceiling for Antigravity CLI practical RE. This is not a successful auth-session trace — it records where no-account / temp-HOME runs stop before model, MCP tool, or cascade execution.

Version pin: 36_installed_cli_version_manifest.md — agy 1.0.12+

Evidence sources (prior passes)

Pass File What it proves
Print + plugin install 31_agy_print_activation_trace.md LS + project + cascade backend init → OAuth timeout before model response
Project / permission flags 38_ay_project_flags_trace.md --project, --new-project, 1.0.13 regex: rules at installed-help tier
Plugin manifest 22_agy_plugin_manifest_trace.md validate/install/list on temp plugin

Confirmed pre-auth startup (from 31)

With temp HOME + --print + installed temp plugin:

  1. Language server listens on localhost HTTPS/HTTP ports
  2. Temp project UUID written under ~/.gemini/config/projects/
  3. CLI store/backend created (product=antigravity, cascadeManager=true, codeAssist=true)
  4. Print mode enters auth wait → times out without OAuth completion

Stdout pattern (abridged): authentication-required prompt, wait-for-authentication, authorization-code prompt, authentication timeout. Per-run OAuth URLs are not recorded (contain session state).

Auth-gated capabilities (not observable without login)

Capability Binary/help evidence Runtime-trace status
Model / cascade response --print, Bubble Tea TUI blocked at OAuth (31)
Plugin MCP tools/list in live session mcp_config.json in plugin not reached post-auth
Subagent scheduling binary strings no public CLI; session-only
Browser/search tools in standalone CLI binary strings IDE-linked context unverified

What remains valid without auth

  • agy --help, agy changelog (installed-help)
  • agy plugin validate|install|list on synthetic plugin (temp HOME)
  • Mach-O strings (binary-string)
  • Changelog-cited flag semantics (38)

Conclusion

Auth-session trace for ay is BLOCKED without user OAuth in a disposable or real account. Corpus must label ay MCP/subagent/cascade runtime semantics as:

  • bundle-key / binary-string when only binary proves existence
  • runtime-trace only after documented authenticated session (not attempted here)

Related

  • RE hub: 15_ay_reverse.md
  • Evidence policy: 00_reverse_evidence_policy.md

015_reverse-engineering/21_synthetic_agent_trace.md

Synthetic Agent Trace

This pass traces public agent/subagent definition surfaces without running model prompts.

Grok Build

Public help trace:

  • /Users/jun/.local/bin/grok --help
  • /Users/jun/.local/bin/grok inspect --help

Findings:

Surface Result
Agent selection --agent <NAME> accepts an agent name or definition file path
Inline subagents --agents <JSON> accepts inline subagent definitions as JSON
Disable subagents --no-subagents disables spawning
Inspect grok inspect --json is the public discovery-state command

Runtime gap: no model prompt was run, so child scheduling, parent history inheritance, and permission inheritance remain untraced.

Cursor Agent

Public help trace:

  • /Users/jun/.local/bin/cursor-agent --help

Findings:

Surface Result
Main entry agent [prompt...] starts Cursor Agent
Modes --mode plan, --mode ask, --plan
Workspace --workspace <path>
Plugin repeatable --plugin-dir <path>
Worktree --worktree, --worktree-base, --skip-worktree-setup

Cursor has bundle-level ./src/subagent/* evidence, but no public subagent list/create/validate command was exposed in this trace.

Kiro CLI

Public help trace:

  • /Users/jun/.local/bin/kiro-cli-chat agent create --help
  • /Users/jun/.local/bin/kiro-cli-chat agent validate --help
  • safe local example: /Users/jun/.kiro/agents/agent_config.json.example

Synthetic validation:

  • temp file: <tmp>/ws/kiro_agent.json
  • command: HOME=<tmp>/home /Users/jun/.local/bin/kiro-cli-chat agent validate --path <tmp>/ws/kiro_agent.json
  • result: exit code 0 with no output

Validated synthetic config fields:

  • name
  • description
  • prompt
  • mcpServers
  • tools
  • toolAliases
  • allowedTools
  • resources
  • hooks
  • toolsSettings
  • includeMcpJson
  • model

Kiro's agent schema is therefore public-doc plus synthetic-validation evidence, not just binary strings.

Follow-up trace /Users/jun/Developer/codex/015_reverse-engineering/24_ki_agent_mcp_trace.md confirms that a workspace agent JSON at .kiro/agents/trace.json is discovered by agent list, and mcp add --agent trace writes a disabled /bin/echo server into that agent's mcpServers object.

Runtime gap: validation does not prove task execution, subagent scheduling, isolation, or tool-permission inheritance.

AGY CLI

Public help trace:

  • /Users/jun/.local/bin/agy --help

Findings:

Surface Result
Public agent command group none exposed
Workspace repeatable --add-dir
Session --continue, --conversation
Plugin plugin / plugins command group

AGY has binary-level SubagentSpec, InvokeSubagentHandler, and BrowserSubagentHandler evidence, but no public standalone subagent definition/validation command was exposed in this trace.

Remaining Gaps

  • Grok inline --agents <JSON> schema validation with a synthetic no-op prompt.
  • Cursor plugin/subagent definition validation without model execution.
  • Kiro actual delegated child execution behavior.
  • AGY hidden subagent activation path.

015_reverse-engineering/22_agy_plugin_manifest_trace.md

AGY Plugin Manifest Trace

This pass used disposable temp HOME and temp plugin directories. It did not import anything into the user's real Antigravity config, run model prompts, read private conversations, or call remote services.

Public Router Behavior

Public commands:

  • /Users/jun/.local/bin/agy plugin --help
  • /Users/jun/.local/bin/agy plugin validate [path]
  • /Users/jun/.local/bin/agy plugin install <target>
  • /Users/jun/.local/bin/agy plugin list

Observed caveat:

  • agy plugin validate --help does not show help. It treats --help as a path and fails with missing plugin.json: stat --help/plugin.json.
  • agy plugin import --help also treats --help as an import source/path and fails with unknown import source or invalid path: --help.

Manifest Minimum

Synthetic validation results:

plugin.json shape Result
{} fails: plugin.json missing name
{"name":"trace-plugin"} passes
name plus version, description, displayName, author passes
name plus top-level skills: [], agents: [] passes, but arrays are not what the validator counts

Conclusion: name is the only confirmed required plugin.json field in this trace. Additional metadata is accepted, but this pass does not prove it is required or semantically consumed.

Component Directory Shapes

Synthetic plugin directories were validated one at a time:

Component Shape Validator result
Skill skills/trace/SKILL.md skills: 1 processed
Agent markdown agents/trace.md agents: 1 processed
Agent JSON file agents/trace.json agents: 1 processed
Agent JSON directory agents/trace/agent.json agents: 1 processed
Command markdown commands/trace.md commands: 1 processed (converted to skills)
Hook JSON hooks/trace.json hooks: 1 processed
MCP config mcp_config.json with mcpServers root mcpServers: 1 processed

Negative shape checks:

Shape Result
top-level mcpServers object inside plugin.json not processed
top-level mcp_servers object inside plugin.json not processed
top-level mcp.json not processed
top-level mcpServers.json not processed
config/mcp_config.json not processed
mcpServers/dummy.json not processed

Install Trace

Command:

  • HOME=<tmp>/home /Users/jun/.local/bin/agy plugin install <tmp>/plugin

Observed write target under temp HOME:

  • <tmp>/home/.gemini/config/import_manifest.json
  • <tmp>/home/.gemini/config/plugins/trace-plugin/plugin.json
  • component files copied under <tmp>/home/.gemini/config/plugins/trace-plugin/...

agy plugin list in the same temp HOME returned JSON:

{
  "imports": [
    {
      "name": "trace-plugin",
      "source": "local-install",
      "components": ["installed"]
    }
  ]
}

Implications:

  • Installed plugin target matches the changelog claim that plugins install to shared ~/.gemini/config/.
  • plugin list is machine-readable JSON in this trace.
  • The list command records the plugin as source: local-install; detailed component inventory is not exposed by plugin list here.

Documentation Consequences

  • AGY's public command surface is narrow, but its plugin validator exposes a concrete extension layout for skills, agents, commands, hooks, and MCP config.
  • commands/*.md are converted into skills, so command and skill surfaces should not be modeled as fully separate runtimes.
  • AGY MCP remains non-public as a standalone agy mcp command group, but plugin-level mcp_config.json with mcpServers is synthetic-validation evidence for MCP extension loading.

Remaining Gaps

  • Print-mode startup with an installed temp plugin is traced in /Users/jun/Developer/codex/015_reverse-engineering/31_agy_print_activation_trace.md; actual post-auth plugin activation inside a live model/cascade session remains untested.
  • Agent execution scheduling from installed plugin agents.
  • Hook trigger names and lifecycle semantics.
  • MCP protocol startup from a plugin-installed mcp_config.json.
  • Marketplace zip trust/signature behavior.

015_reverse-engineering/24_ki_agent_mcp_trace.md

Kiro Agent MCP Trace

This pass used disposable temp HOME and workspace directories. It did not run model prompts, start real MCP servers, authenticate, or read private Kiro sessions.

Public Help Surface

Commands inspected:

  • /Users/jun/.local/bin/kiro-cli-chat agent create --help
  • /Users/jun/.local/bin/kiro-cli-chat agent list --help
  • /Users/jun/.local/bin/kiro-cli-chat mcp add --help
  • /Users/jun/.local/bin/kiro-cli-chat mcp list --help

Relevant help semantics:

  • agent create [NAME] --directory <DIRECTORY> creates an agent config in the chosen directory, but opens an editor by default.
  • agent list says local agents are discovered only when invoked in a directory that contains them.
  • mcp add --agent <AGENT> writes the server to the named agent.
  • mcp add --scope <SCOPE> is only meaningful when no agent name is supplied.

Editor Behavior

agent create trace --directory <tmp>/ws/.kiro/agents created <tmp>/ws/.kiro/agents/trace.json and opened it in vi. The generated template shape was:

{
  "name": "trace",
  "description": "",
  "prompt": null,
  "mcpServers": {},
  "tools": ["*"],
  "toolAliases": {},
  "allowedTools": [],
  "resources": [],
  "hooks": {},
  "toolsSettings": {},
  "includeMcpJson": true,
  "model": null
}

The editor process was terminated after capturing the template. Follow-up traces used a directly written synthetic JSON file with the same shape to avoid interactive editor state.

Workspace Agent Discovery

Synthetic file:

  • <tmp>/ws/.kiro/agents/trace.json

Commands:

  • HOME=<tmp>/home /Users/jun/.local/bin/kiro-cli-chat agent validate --path <tmp>/ws/.kiro/agents/trace.json
  • HOME=<tmp>/home /Users/jun/.local/bin/kiro-cli-chat agent list

Findings:

Surface Result
validation exit code 0, no output
agent list shows built-ins plus trace as Workspace
built-ins kiro_default, kiro_help, kiro_planner remain visible

Agent-Specific MCP Routing

Command:

  • HOME=<tmp>/home /Users/jun/.local/bin/kiro-cli-chat mcp add --name dummy --agent trace --command /bin/echo --args dummy --disabled --force

Observed output:

  • Added MCP server 'dummy' to agent trace

mcp list then grouped the server under workspace agent trace:

workspace:
  kiro_help
    (empty)
  kiro_planner
    (empty)
  trace
    - dummy        /bin/echo (disabled)

mcp status --name dummy reported:

Field Result
Scope workspace
Agent trace
Command /bin/echo
Timeout 120000 ms
Disabled true

The synthetic agent JSON was updated in place:

"mcpServers": {
  "dummy": {
    "command": "/bin/echo",
    "args": ["dummy"],
    "env": {},
    "disabled": true
  }
}

Documentation Consequences

  • Kiro has a public no-model path for validating custom workspace agents and binding MCP servers to a specific agent.
  • --agent <name> overrides --scope; agent-specific MCP config is stored inside the agent JSON rather than the workspace .kiro/settings/mcp.json path observed for non-agent workspace MCP.
  • includeMcpJson: true is present in the generated agent template, but this trace does not prove its runtime inclusion semantics.

Official Server Status Follow-up

A second temp workspace tested an enabled @modelcontextprotocol/server-everything config without a model session:

  • temp HOME: /tmp/kiro-mcp-official.anATMg/home
  • temp workspace: /tmp/kiro-mcp-official.anATMg/ws
  • config file: /tmp/kiro-mcp-official.anATMg/ws/.kiro/settings/mcp.json

Command:

HOME=/tmp/kiro-mcp-official.anATMg/home /Users/jun/.local/bin/kiro-cli-chat mcp add --name everything --scope workspace --command /Users/jun/.nvm/versions/node/v24.14.1/bin/npx --args '["-y","@modelcontextprotocol/server-everything"]' --env npm_config_cache=/tmp/kiro-mcp-official.anATMg/npm-cache --force

Result:

✓ Added MCP server 'everything' to workspace config in /private/tmp/kiro-mcp-official.anATMg/ws/.kiro/settings/mcp.json

The written config used:

{"mcpServers":{"everything":{"command":"/Users/jun/.nvm/versions/node/v24.14.1/bin/npx","args":["-y","@modelcontextprotocol/server-everything"],"env":{"npm_config_cache":"/tmp/kiro-mcp-official.anATMg/npm-cache"}}}}

mcp status --name everything -vv did not report protocol handshake or tool discovery. It reported:

  • not logged in, no valid token found
  • workspace config path /private/tmp/kiro-mcp-official.anATMg/ws/.kiro
  • global config path /tmp/kiro-mcp-official.anATMg/home/.kiro
  • scope displayed as default
  • agent displayed as kiro_default
  • command path to npx
  • timeout 120000 ms
  • disabled false
  • env var npm_config_cache=/tmp/kiro-mcp-official.anATMg/npm-cache

Remaining Gaps

  • Actual MCP server startup and protocol handshake.
  • Agent-specific MCP behavior during a model session.
  • Interaction between includeMcpJson, global/workspace MCP config, and agent-local mcpServers.
  • Custom agent execution, delegation, and tool-permission inheritance.

015_reverse-engineering/32_agy_agentapi_route_trace.md

AGY AgentAPI Route Trace

This pass inspected the installed AGY binary and public wrapper only. It did not authenticate, read private AGY logs, read private conversations, or send Agent API messages.

Wrapper Identity

Only one installed Agent API wrapper was found under the Antigravity CLI home:

/Users/jun/.gemini/antigravity-cli/bin/agentapi

file classified it as:

/Users/jun/.gemini/antigravity-cli/bin/agentapi: POSIX shell script text executable, ASCII text
/Users/jun/.local/bin/agy:                           Mach-O 64-bit executable arm64
/Users/jun/.gemini/antigravity-cli/bin/webm_encoder: Mach-O 64-bit executable arm64

The wrapper contents are:

#!/bin/sh
exec "/Users/jun/.local/bin/agy" agentapi "$@"

This proves that AGY installs an agentapi executable shim, but not that the public top-level router currently accepts agentapi.

Public Router Behavior

Direct public route:

/Users/jun/.local/bin/agy agentapi --help

returned exit 0, but printed only top-level AGY help. The available subcommands listed were:

changelog
help
install
plugin
plugins
update

No agentapi subcommand help, handler list, or Agent API command schema was exposed.

Explicit help lookup:

/Users/jun/.local/bin/agy help agentapi

returned exit 1 with:

Error: unknown subcommand: agentapi

The wrapper path behaves the same because it execs /Users/jun/.local/bin/agy agentapi "$@". Therefore the current installed public CLI surface has a wrapper/script for agentapi, but the direct public subcommand route is not active in normal no-context invocation.

Binary String Evidence

The AGY Mach-O binary still contains first-class Agent API implementation strings and prompt templates. Relevant hits include:

usage: agentapi %s %s
failed to write agentapi script
failed to format agentapi script
exec "%s" agentapi "$@"
agentapi new-conversation "Help me fix this bug"
agentapi get-conversation-metadata "some-conversation-id"
agentapi new-conversation --model=%s "Run a complex refactor"
agentapi send-message "<other-conversation-id>" "Task completed successfully"
agentapi send-message "<your-conversation-id>" "Reminder: check build status"
You have access to the Agent API CLI tool: 'agentapi', which contains subcommands for interacting with Agent API.
subprocess.run(["agentapi", subcommand, arg1, arg2])
- **events/** (Generated): For every agentapi call, a timestamped .json file is created in the events/ subdirectory.
- Background sidecars can use the agentapi CLI tool to programmatically interact with the system.
google3/third_party/jetski/cortex/agentapi/agentapi.RegisterHandler
google3/third_party/jetski/cortex/agentapi/agentapi.(*sendMessageHandler).Execute
google3/third_party/jetski/cortex/agentapi/agentapi.(*newConversationHandler).Execute
google3/third_party/jetski/cortex/agentapi/agentapi.(*getConversationMetadataHandler).Execute
google3/third_party/jetski/cortex/agentapi/agentapi.(*sendMessageHandler).Metadata
google3/third_party/jetski/cortex/agentapi/agentapi.(*newConversationHandler).Metadata
google3/third_party/jetski/cortex/agentapi/agentapi.(*getConversationMetadataHandler).Metadata
google3/third_party/jetski/cortex/agentapi/agentapi.GetHandlerNames
google3/third_party/jetski/cortex/agentapi/agentapi.GetHandler

This changes the conclusion from "agentapi unknown" to "Agent API implementation exists in the binary, but the installed public wrapper does not expose it as a normal subcommand without the right runtime context."

MCP / Browser Linkage

The same binary contains browser/MCP bridge strings adjacent to Agent API sidecar strings:

McpBrowserRecordingStopHook: Failed to send agentapi message (output: %s)
google3/third_party/jetski/cortex/executors/posttoolhooks/posttoolhooks.NewMcpBrowserRecordingStopHook
/exa.extension_server_pb.ExtensionServerService/GetChromeDevtoolsMcpUrl
GetChromeDevtoolsMcpUrlRequest
GetChromeDevtoolsMcpUrlResponse
GetChromeDevtoolsMcpUrl
RefreshMcpServers
ToggleMcpServer
GetMcpServerStates
CompleteMcpOAuth

This is binary-level evidence that Agent API, browser recording hooks, Chrome DevTools MCP URL plumbing, and MCP server state/OAuth controls are connected inside AGY's runtime. It is not a proof that a no-auth standalone shell can call those paths.

Interpretation

AGY has two separable surfaces:

Surface Status
Installed wrapper confirmed at /Users/jun/.gemini/antigravity-cli/bin/agentapi
Direct public CLI route not exposed in normal no-context invocation; help agentapi says unknown
Binary Agent API implementation confirmed by handler, command, prompt-template, sidecar, and generated-events strings
Known subcommand names from strings send-message, new-conversation, get-conversation-metadata
Working command invocation not confirmed without authenticated/runtime context

The most likely model is that AGY writes or enables the agentapi shim for background sidecars or agent runtime contexts, while normal top-level agy agentapi is not a documented public command in the current installed build.

Named Subcommand Help Probe

A disposable HOME probe through the installed wrapper confirmed that known binary subcommand names still do not expose public help:

HOME=/tmp/... /Users/jun/.gemini/antigravity-cli/bin/agentapi send-message --help
HOME=/tmp/... /Users/jun/.gemini/antigravity-cli/bin/agentapi new-conversation --help
HOME=/tmp/... /Users/jun/.gemini/antigravity-cli/bin/agentapi get-conversation-metadata --help

All three exited 0 but printed only top-level AGY help with the public subcommand list changelog, help, install, plugin, plugins, and update. This tightens the boundary: send-message, new-conversation, and get-conversation-metadata are binary handler names, not no-context public CLI routes in this install.

Sidecar Contract Strings

The binary also exposes a sidecar configuration/runtime vocabulary adjacent to Agent API examples:

  • sidecar root examples: sidecars/, sidecar_data/, one folder per sidecar ID, required sidecar.json
  • config fields: command, builtin, restart_policy, args, description, has_web_ui, env, display_name, disabled, ui_config
  • runtime directories: data/, logs/, events/
  • runtime environment: ANTIGRAVITY_EXECUTABLE_DATA_DIR, ANTIGRAVITY_SIDECAR_WEB_PORT
  • builtin scheduler shape: schedule with args like ["<cron_schedule>", "<command>", "<arg1>", ...]
  • schedule syntax examples: five-field cron, @hourly, @daily, @every 1h, @every 30m
  • schema names: SidecarUserConfig, SidecarStatusInfo, SidecarUIConfig, SidecarUIView, SidecarUIEntrypoint

This supports the interpretation that Agent API is intended for sidecar/runtime contexts. It still does not expose the activation environment or output schema for safe no-context calls.

Remaining Gaps

  • Whether agentapi becomes active only with specific environment variables, app-data directory state, sidecar context, or authenticated project runtime.
  • Exact arguments and JSON output schema for send-message, new-conversation, and get-conversation-metadata.
  • Whether events/*.json are written under a temp runtime directory, an app-data directory, or a conversation-specific directory.
  • Whether background scheduled tasks can safely invoke agentapi from PATH without going through agy agentapi.
  • Whether McpBrowserRecordingStopHook sends messages via Agent API only after a live browser/MCP recording session.
  • Whether Agent API is stable user-facing contract or internal sidecar contract.

015_reverse-engineering/38_ay_project_flags_trace.md

AGY Project Flags And Permission Rules Trace

Disposable evidence pass for agy 1.0.12+ launch and permission changelog items. No OAuth session, no conversation store reads, no private settings values.

Version pin: /Users/jun/Developer/codex/015_reverse-engineering/36_installed_cli_version_manifest.md — agy 1.0.12 (agy --version), changelog through 1.0.13.

Installed-help — launch flags

Command: /Users/jun/.local/bin/agy --help (2026-06-27)

Flag Help text (abridged)
--project Project ID for the current CLI session
--new-project Create a new project for this session
--print / -p Run a single prompt non-interactively and print the response
--print-timeout Timeout for print mode wait (default 5m0s)
--sandbox Run in a sandbox with terminal restrictions enabled
--dangerously-skip-permissions Auto-approve all tool permission requests without prompting

Public subcommands remain: changelog, help, install, models, plugin/plugins, update.

Safe-local-doc — changelog 1.0.12–1.0.13

Source: agy changelog + /Users/jun/.gemini/antigravity-cli/cache/CHANGELOG.md

Version Claim Evidence class
1.0.12 --project, --new-project launch flags; project resolution defaults regardless of workspace installed-help + changelog
1.0.12 Project config in ~/.gemini/config/projects/ overrides global ~/.gemini/antigravity-cli/settings.json safe-local-doc (changelog path names only)
1.0.13 “Always Approve” rules strict by default; opt-in regex via regex: prefix changelog
1.0.13 Safe commands with output redirection may match without full-command approval changelog

Binary-string — resume hint

strings /Users/jun/.local/bin/agy (2026-06-27) includes:

Resume in the same project: agy --conversation=%s --project=%s

Confirms --project pairs with --conversation in exit/resume UX strings (not a separate public subcommand).

Binary also contains regex: adjacent to permission-rule copy (minified bundle fragment); aligns with 1.0.13 changelog — not a standalone CLI flag.

Synthetic boundary (print mode, no auth)

Not re-run in this pass: full --print OAuth cascade. Prior boundary unchanged in 31_agy_print_activation_trace.md (language server + store init → OAuth timeout before model response).

New claim allowed: --project / --new-project are installed-help confirmed; runtime project-ID assignment semantics still need authenticated --print or interactive session (runtime-trace pending).

Documentation consequences

  • Axis docs citing only 1.0.3 project behavior → upgrade to manifest pin + this trace.
  • Permission “regex approve” → cite 1.0.13 changelog + binary regex: string; do not claim default is regex.

Related

  • RE hub: 15_ay_reverse.md
  • Print activation: 31_agy_print_activation_trace.md
  • Agent API routes: 32_agy_agentapi_route_trace.md
37

기술 참조 & 합성

20-80 + grand synthesis

기초 설명

  • 2026-03-26: Antigravity 에이전트 시스템 동작 원리를 15개 섹션으로 상세 분석. 시스템 기동 시퀀스, Cascade 세션 생명주기, Mixin 프롬프트 조립, LLM 호출과 도구 디스패치, Trajectory/Step 관리, 코드 편집과 diff zone, 명령 실행과 보안, Executor 3-tier, Implicit Trajectory, Knowledge 시스템, MCP 통합, 에러 처리와 이중 교정 루프, 사용자 상호작용, 세션 종료와 후처리, 전체 에이전트 루프 요약 포함.

이 문서에서 다루는 것

  • 변경 기록
  • 변경 기록
  • 000/00_grand_synthesis.md (발췌)
    1. 개요
  • 이 문서의 목적
  • 분석 범위
  • 분석 방법론
  • 이 문서를 읽는 방법
    1. 에이전트 프로필 요약
  • Codex
  • Aider

소스 코드 스니펫

README / docs

graph TD
    A[5개 에이전트 개요] --> B[13개 차원 비교]
    B --> C[공통 패턴 추출]
    B --> D[핵심 차별점 추출]
    C --> E[기술 선택 시사점]
    D --> E
    E --> F[미래 전망과 설계 교훈]

코퍼스 — 000_overview/00_ag_overview.md (심화)

변경 기록

변경 기록

  • 2026-03-26: Antigravity 에이전트 시스템 동작 원리를 15개 섹션으로 상세 분석. 시스템 기동 시퀀스, Cascade 세션 생명주기, Mixin 프롬프트 조립, LLM 호출과 도구 디스패치, Trajectory/Step 관리, 코드 편집과 diff zone, 명령 실행과 보안, Executor 3-tier, Implicit Trajectory, Knowledge 시스템, MCP 통합, 에러 처리와 이중 교정 루프, 사용자 상호작용, 세션 종료와 후처리, 전체 에이전트 루프 요약 포함.

000/00_grand_synthesis.md (발췌)

GRAND SYNTHESIS — 5개 코딩 에이전트 × 13개 차원 최종 종합 분석

이 문서는 Codex, Aider, Gemini CLI, OpenCode, Claude Code를 13개 아키텍처 차원에서 동시에 비교한 캡스톤 문서다. 개별 비교 문서의 결론을 단순 재배열하지 않고, 서로 다른 설계 철학이 어떤 제품 전략으로 수렴하는지까지 읽어내는 것을 목표로 한다.

graph TD
    A[5개 에이전트 개요] --> B[13개 차원 비교]
    B --> C[공통 패턴 추출]
    B --> D[핵심 차별점 추출]
    C --> E[기술 선택 시사점]
    D --> E
    E --> F[미래 전망과 설계 교훈]

1. 개요

이 문서의 목적

이 문서의 목적은 5개 에이전트를 한 번에 조망할 수 있는 상위 레퍼런스를 만드는 것이다. 개별 문서는 특정 축의 우열을 설명하지만, 실제 제품 선택은 언제나 여러 축의 동시 판단을 요구한다. 따라서 여기서는 TUI, prompt, tool, skill, subagent, app-server, sandbox, config, MCP, memory, plugin, guardian, 그리고 잔여 교차 관심사를 하나의 프레임으로 다시 묶는다.

  • 근거: [[00_co_overview]], [[00_ai_overview]], [[00_ge_overview]], [[00_oc_overview]], [[00_cc_overview]]

분석 범위

범위는 5개 에이전트와 13개 차원이다. 에이전트는 Codex, Aider, Gemini CLI, OpenCode, Claude Code다. 차원은 TUI, Prompt/Context, Tool Runtime, Skill, Subagents, App-Server Surface, Sandbox, Config, MCP, Memory, Plugin, Guardian, 잔여 교차 관심사(Git/VCS, 모델 라우팅, 에러 처리, 텔레메트리, IDE 통합, 패키징, 테스트, 인증, 멀티모달, 이벤트 시스템, 피처 플래그, 국제화)다. App-Server 축은 비교 문서 자체가 Codex·Gemini CLI·OpenCode 중심으로 작성되어 있으므로 Aider와 Claude Code는 “의도적 부재”까지 함께 해석한다.

  • 근거: [[001_tui-design/00_tui]], [[002_prompt-context/00_prompt]], [[003_tool-runtime/00_tool]], [[004_skill-system/00_skill]], [[005_subagents-collab/00_subagent]], [[006_app-server-client-surfaces/00_surface]], [[007_sandbox-exec-policy/00_sandbox]], [[008_config-system/00_config]], [[009_mcp-orchestration/00_mcp]], [[010_memory-pipeline/00_memory]], [[011_plugin-system/00_plugin]], [[012_guardian-approval/00_guardian]], [[013_etc/00_etc]]

분석 방법론

첫째, 각 에이전트 개요 문서에서 기술 스택, 패키지 구조, 핵심 철학을 추출했다. 둘째, 13개 비교 문서에서 축별 우위와 결손, 스카우팅 점수, 디자인 패턴을 수집했다. 셋째, 같은 에이전트가 여러 축에서 반복적으로 보이는 패턴을 묶어 장기 전략으로 재해석했다. 넷째, 단일 승자를 찾기보다 “어떤 상황에서 어떤 설계가 더 설득력 있는가”를 기준으로 실무 추천안을 도출했다.

  • 근거: [[00_co_overview]], [[00_ai_overview]], [[00_ge_overview]], [[00_oc_overview]], [[00_cc_overview]], [[001_tui-design/00_tui]], [[012_guardian-approval/00_guardian]]

이 문서를 읽는 방법

제품 선택이 목적이면 3장, 7장, 8장을 먼저 보는 것이 좋다. 포크나 재구현이 목적이면 2장, 5장, 6장, 9장을 먼저 보는 것이 좋다. 보안 검토가 목적이면 3장, 4장, 8장, 9장을 함께 읽는 편이 낫다. 마지막 부록은 문서 인덱스 역할을 하므로 세부 원문으로 돌아갈 때 사용하면 된다.

  • 근거: [[007_sandbox-exec-policy/00_sandbox]], [[009_mcp-orchestration/00_mcp]], [[011_plugin-system/00_plugin]], [[012_guardian-approval/00_guardian]]

2. 에이전트 프로필 요약

에이전트 기술 스택 설계 철학 핵심 차별점 실무 적합도 근거 문서
Codex Rust + TypeScript + React/Ink + app-server protocol 엔진을 층으로 분리하고, prompt·tool·skill·subagent·MCP를 제어면(control plane)으로 다룬다. 가장 분해 가능한 core engine, 가장 강한 terminal-native orchestration, 가장 균형 잡힌 sandbox/MCP 결합. 보안, 오케스트레이션, 포크 가능한 core engine이 중요한 팀 [[00_co_overview]], [[001_tui-design/00_tui]], [[002_prompt-context/00_prompt]]
Aider Python + prompt_toolkit + rich + tree-sitter + Git 도구 플랫폼보다 코드 편집 루프에 집중한다. 모델을 좋은 editor로 만드는 쪽에 최적화한다. RepoMap, edit 전략 다양성, git auto-commit, 최소 마찰 CLI 경험. 개인 개발자, 빠른 코드 편집, 낮은 학습 비용이 중요한 사용 사례 [[00_ai_overview]], [[001_tui-design/00_tui]], [[002_prompt-context/00_prompt]]
Gemini CLI TypeScript + React/Ink + Zod + A2A server + multi-package monorepo schema-first와 policy-first를 결합해 CLI를 확장 운영체계처럼 다룬다. PromptProvider/Scheduler/PolicyEngine/ModelRouter/ExtensionManager가 분리된 가장 운영형 TypeScript engine. 엔터프라이즈 CLI, 확장 운영, 정책 감사, TypeScript 기반 재구현 [[00_ge_overview]], [[001_tui-design/00_tui]], [[004_skill-system/00_skill]]
OpenCode TypeScript + Bun + Effect-TS + Hono + SolidJS + Tauri agent runtime을 웹/데스크톱/SDK가 공유하는 product platform으로 본다. Effect-TS service layer, 21+ provider, plugin SDK, session replay, multi-surface delivery. 빠른 제품화, GUI/desktop 포함, TS로 직접 확장하고 싶은 팀 [[00_oc_overview]], [[001_tui-design/00_tui]], [[003_tool-runtime/00_tool]]
Claude Code TypeScript/JavaScript bundle + React/Ink + custom renderer + Anthropic runtime 제품 사용성을 최우선으로 하면서 hook, security chain, memory, MCP를 깊게 묶는다. 가장 풍부한 productized tool surface, deepest shell security, strongest file-based memory UX. 상용 CLI UX, 강한 shell security, memory-heavy workflows, enterprise policy integration [[00_cc_overview]], [[001_tui-design/00_tui]], [[005_subagents-collab/00_subagent]]

Codex

  • 정체성: 엔진을 층으로 분리하고, prompt·tool·skill·subagent·MCP를 제어면(control plane)으로 다룬다.
  • 기술 스택: Rust + TypeScript + React/Ink + app-server protocol
  • 핵심 차별점: 가장 분해 가능한 core engine, 가장 강한 terminal-native orchestration, 가장 균형 잡힌 sandbox/MCP 결합.
  • 실무에서 강한 상황: 보안, 오케스트레이션, 포크 가능한 core engine이 중요한 팀
  • 재구현 관점 한 줄 평: requirements 제약 채널과 prompt fragment provenance를 분리한 구조.
  • 근거: [[00_co_overview]], [[001_tui-design/00_tui]], [[002_prompt-context/00_prompt]], [[003_tool-runtime/00_tool]], [[005_subagents-collab/00_subagent]], [[007_sandbox-exec-policy/00_sandbox]], [[009_mcp-orchestration/00_mcp]]

Aider

  • 정체성: 도구 플랫폼보다 코드 편집 루프에 집중한다. 모델을 좋은 editor로 만드는 쪽에 최적화한다.
  • 기술 스택: Python + prompt_toolkit + rich + tree-sitter + Git
  • 핵심 차별점: RepoMap, edit 전략 다양성, git auto-com

AG101 · 코퍼스 근거 · 업데이트 2026-06-28
